info.description: The Gemini Interactions API is an experimental API that allows developers to build generative AI applications using Gemini models. Gemini is our most capable model, built from the ground up to be multimodal. It can generalize and seamlessly understand, operate across, and combine different types of information including language, images, audio, video, and code. You can use the Gemini API for use cases like reasoning across text and images, content generation, dialogue agents, summarization and classification systems, and more.
info.title: Gemini API

content.text:
Hello! I'm functioning perfectly and ready to assist you.

How are you doing today?
The capital of France is Paris.
A white humanoid robot with glowing blue eyes stands holding a red skateboard.
Here is a comprehensive research report on the current state of cancer research...

x-codeSamples.label: simple
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/interactions \
  -H "x-goog-api-key: $GEMINI_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Api-Revision: 2026-05-20" \
  -d '{
    "model": "gemini-3-flash-preview",
    "input": "Hello, how are you?"
  }'

x-codeSamples.label: simple
x-codeSamples.source:
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()
interaction = client.interactions.create(
    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",
    input="Hello, how are you?",
)
print(interaction.output_text)

x-codeSamples.label: simple
multi_turn
multi_turn
x-codeSamples.source:
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()
response = client.interactions.create(
    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",
    input=[
        { "type": "user_input", "content": [{ "type": "text", "text": "Hello!" }] },
        { "type": "model_output", "content": [{ "type": "text", "text": "Hi there! How can I help you today?" }] },
        { "type": "user_input", "content": [{ "type": "text", "text": "What is the capital of France?" }] }
    ]
)
print(response.output_text)

client = genai.Client()

get.description: Retrieves the full details of a single interaction based on its `Interaction.id`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the interaction to retrieve.

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: If set to true, the generated content will be streamed incrementally.

parameters.name: last_event_id
parameters.description: Optional. If set, resumes the interaction stream from the next chunk after the event marked by the event id. Can only be used if `stream` is true.

parameters.name: include_input
parameters.description: If set to true, includes the input in the response.

content.text: I'm doing great, thank you for asking! How can I help you today?

x-codeSamples.label: get
x-codeSamples.source:
# [setup]
INTERACTION_ID=$(curl -s -X POST https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/interactions \
  -H "x-goog-api-key: $GEMINI_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Api-Revision: 2026-05-20" \
  -d '{"model": "gemini-3-flash-preview", "input": "Say hello."}' \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")
# [/setup]

curl -X GET "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/interactions/$INTERACTION_ID" \
  -H "x-goog-api-key: $GEMINI_API_KEY" \
  -H "Api-Revision: 2026-05-20"

x-codeSamples.label: get
x-codeSamples.source:
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()

# [setup]
created = client.interactions.create(
    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",
    input="Say hello."
)
# [/setup]

interaction = client.interactions.get(id=created.id)
print(interaction.status)

x-codeSamples.label: get

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the interaction to delete.

x-codeSamples.label: delete
x-codeSamples.source:
# [setup]
INTERACTION_ID=$(curl -s -X POST https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/interactions \
  -H "x-goog-api-key: $GEMINI_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Api-Revision: 2026-05-20" \
  -d '{"model": "gemini-3-flash-preview", "input": "Hello"}' \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")
# [/setup]

curl -X DELETE "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/interactions/$INTERACTION_ID" \
  -H "x-goog-api-key: $GEMINI_API_KEY" \
  -H "Api-Revision: 2026-05-20"

x-codeSamples.label: delete
x-codeSamples.source:
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()

# [setup]
created = client.interactions.create(
    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",
    input="Hello",
)
# [/setup]

client.interactions.delete(id=created.id)
print("Interaction deleted successfully.")

x-codeSamples.label: delete

post.description: Cancels an interaction by id. This only applies to background interactions that are still running.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the interaction to cancel.

x-codeSamples.label: cancel
x-codeSamples.source:
# [setup]
INTERACTION_ID=$(curl -s -X POST https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/interactions \
  -H "x-goog-api-key: $GEMINI_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Api-Revision: 2026-05-20" \
  -d '{"model": "gemini-3-flash-preview", "input": "Write a long essay about the history of computing.", "background": true}' \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")
# [/setup]

curl -X POST "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/interactions/$INTERACTION_ID/cancel" \
  -H "x-goog-api-key: $GEMINI_API_KEY" \
  -H "Api-Revision: 2026-05-20"

x-codeSamples.label: cancel
x-codeSamples.source:
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()

# Start a background interaction so it stays in-progress.
created = client.interactions.create(
    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",
    input="Write a long essay about the history of computing.",
    tools=[{"type": "computer_use"}],
    background=True,
)

# Cancel the in-progress interaction.
interaction = client.interactions.cancel(id=created.id)
print(interaction.status)

x-codeSamples.label: cancel

parameters.description:
Optional. The maximum number of webhooks to return. The service may return fewer than
this value. If unspecified, at most 50 webhooks will be returned.
The maximum value is 1000.
parameters.name: page_size

Optional. A page token, received from a previous `ListWebhooks` call.
Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.

post.description: Creates a new Agent (Typed version for SDK).

value.system_instruction: You are a helpful research assistant.

x-codeSamples.label: create
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/agents \\
  -H "x-goog-api-key: $GEMINI_API_KEY" \\
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \\
  -H "Api-Revision: 2026-05-20" \\
  -d '{
    "id": "research-assistant-abc123",
    "base_agent": "waverunner",
    "description": "A helpful research assistant.",
    "system_instruction": "You are a helpful research assistant.",
    "base_environment": "remote",
    "tools": [{"type": "google_search"}]
  }'

x-codeSamples.label: create
x-codeSamples.source:
import uuid
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()
agent = client.agents.create(
    id=f"research-assistant-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
    base_agent="waverunner",
    description="A helpful research assistant.",
    system_instruction="You are a helpful research assistant.",
    base_environment="remote",
    tools=[{"type": "google_search"}],
)
print(agent.id)

# [cleanup]
client.agents.delete(agent.id)
# [/cleanup]

x-codeSamples.label: create

data.system_instruction: You are a helpful research assistant.

x-codeSamples.label: list
x-codeSamples.source:

  -H "Api-Revision: 2026-05-20"

x-codeSamples.label: list
x-codeSamples.source:
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()
response = client.agents.list()
for agent in response.agents or []:
    print(agent.id)

x-codeSamples.label: get
x-codeSamples.source:
import uuid
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()

# Create an agent so we have a valid ID to retrieve.
agent_id = f"test-agent-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
client.agents.create(
    id=agent_id,
    base_agent="waverunner",
    description="A test agent.",
    base_environment="remote",
)

agent = client.agents.get(agent_id)
print(agent.id)

# [cleanup]
client.agents.delete(agent.id)
# [/cleanup]

x-codeSamples.label: get

x-codeSamples.source:

  -H "x-goog-api-key: $GEMINI_API_KEY" \\
  -H "Api-Revision: 2026-05-20"

x-codeSamples.label: delete
x-codeSamples.source:
import uuid
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()

# Create an agent so we have a valid ID to delete.
agent_id = f"delete-test-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
client.agents.create(
    id=agent_id,
    base_agent="waverunner",
    description="Temporary agent for deletion.",
    base_environment="remote",
)

client.agents.delete(agent_id)
print("Agent deleted successfully.")

api_version.name: api_version
api_version.description: Which version of the API to use.

Agent.description:
An agent definition for the CreateAgent API.
This message is the target for annotation-parser-based JSON parsing.
New format:
  {
    "id": "customer-sentinel",
    "base_agent": "",
    "system_instruction": "...",
    "base_environment": { "type": "remote", "sources": [...] },
    "tools": [ {"type": "code_execution"} ]
  }

base_environment.description: The environment configuration for the agent.

description.description: Agent description for developers to quickly read and understand.

id.description: The unique identifier for the agent.

tools.description: The tools available to the agent.

AgentTool.description: A tool that the agent can use.

mode.description: The mode of the tool choice.

tools.description: The names of the allowed tools.

mime_type.description: The mime type of the audio.

sample_rate.description: The sample rate of the audio.

rate.description: Deprecated. Use sample_rate instead. The value is ignored.

sample_rate.description: The sample rate of the audio.

bit_rate.description:
Bit rate in bits per second (bps). Only applicable for compressed formats
(MP3, Opus).

delivery.description: The delivery mode for the audio output.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Audio data is returned inline in the response.
Audio data is returned as a URI.

mime_type.description: The MIME type of the audio output.

CodeExecution.description: A tool that can be used by the model to execute code.

    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",

print(response.output_text)

delta.text: Elara’s life was a symphony of quiet moments. A librarian, she found solace in the hushed aisles, the scent of aged paper, and the predictable rhythm of her days. Her small apartment, meticulously ordered, reflected this internal calm, save

DeepResearchAgentConfig.description: Configuration for the Deep Research agent.

collaborative_planning.description:
Enables human-in-the-loop planning for the Deep Research agent. If set to
true, the Deep Research agent will provide a research plan in its response.
The agent will then proceed only if the user confirms the plan in the next
turn.

thinking_summaries.description: Whether to include thought summaries in the response.

visualization.description: Whether to include visualizations in the response.

mime_type.description: The mime type of the document.

additionalProperties.description: For agents that are not supported statically in the API definition.

sources.content: You are a data analyst. Always include visualizations and export results as PDF.

---
name: slide-maker
description: Create HTML slide decks
---
# Slide Maker

When asked to create a presentation:
1. Analyze the input data
2. Create an HTML slide deck with reveal.js
3. Save to /workspace/output/slides.html

oneOf.description: Outbound networking configuration for the sandbox. When specified, restricts which external domains the sandbox can reach. Omit entirely to allow all outbound traffic with no header injection.

allowlist.description: List of allowed outbound domains. Only requests to listed domains are permitted. Use [{'domain': '*'}] to allow all domains while still injecting headers on specific ones.

EnvironmentNetworkEgressAllowlist.description: Outbound networking configuration for the sandbox. Accepts an object with an 'allowlist' array to restrict traffic, or the string 'disabled' to turn off all network access. Omit entirely to allow all outbound traffic with no header injection.

code.description: A URI that identifies the error type.

event_id.description:
The event_id token to be used to resume the interaction stream, from
this event.

error.message: Failed to get completed interaction: Result not found.

custom_metadata.description: User provided metadata about the retrieved context.

end_index.description: End of the attributed segment, exclusive.

media_id.description: Media ID in-case of image citations, if applicable.

page_number.description: Page number of the cited document, if applicable.

source.description: Source attributed for a portion of the text.

start_index.description:
Start of segment of the response that is attributed to this source.

Index indicates the start of the segment, measured in bytes.

FileSearch.description: A tool that can be used by the model to search files.

file_search_store_names.description: The file search store names to search.

metadata_filter.description: Metadata filter to apply to the semantic retrieval documents and chunks.

top_k.description: The number of semantic retrieval chunks to retrieve.

x-codeSamples.label: file_search
file_search
x-codeSamples.source:
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()

# Create a file search store so we have a valid one to use.
store = client.file_search_stores.create()

response = client.interactions.create(
    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",
    tools=[{
        "type": "file_search",
        "file_search_store_names": [store.name]
    }],
    input="What documents are available?"
)
print(response.output_text)

# [cleanup]
client.file_search_stores.delete(name=store.name)
# [/cleanup]

x-codeSamples.label: file_search

from google import genai

client = genai.Client()
response = client.interactions.create(
    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",
    tools=[{
        "type": "google_maps",
        "latitude": 37.7749,
        "longitude": -122.4194
    }],
    input="What is the best food near me?"
)
print(response.output_text)

McpServer.description: A MCPServer is a server that can be called by the model to perform actions.

Example: "https://api.example.com/mcp"

mcp_server
x-codeSamples.source:
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()
response = client.interactions.create(
    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",
    tools=[{
        "type": "mcp_server",
        "name": "weather_service",
        "url": "https://gemini-api-demos.uc.r.appspot.com/mcp"
    }],
    input="Today is 12-05-2025, what is the temperature today in London?"
)
print(response.output_text)

UrlContext.description: A tool that can be used by the model to fetch URL context.

x-codeSamples.label: url_context
url_context
x-codeSamples.source:
from google import genai

client = genai.Client()
response = client.interactions.create(
    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",
    tools=[{"type": "url_context"}],
    input="Summarize https://www.example.com"
)
print(response.output_text)

x-codeSamples.label: url_context

total_cached_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the cached part of the prompt (the cached content).

total_input_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the prompt (context).

total_output_tokens.description: Total number of tokens across all the generated responses.

total_thought_tokens.description: Number of tokens of thoughts for thinking models.

total_tokens.description:
Total token count for the interaction request (prompt + responses + other
internal tokens).

total_tool_use_tokens.description: Number of tokens present in tool-use prompt(s).

Output only. The environment ID for the interaction. Only populated if environment
config is set in the request.

id.description: Required. Output only. A unique identifier for the interaction completion.

previous_interaction_id.description: The ID of the previous interaction, if any.

response_format.description: Enforces that the generated response is a JSON object that complies with the JSON schema specified in this field.

response_mime_type.description: The mime type of the response. This is required if response_format is set.

response_modalities.description: The requested modalities of the response (TEXT, IMAGE, AUDIO).

role.description: Output only. The role of the interaction.

service_tier.description: The service tier for the interaction.

status.description: Required. Output only. The status of the interaction.

AllowlistEntry.description: A single domain allowlist rule with optional header injection.

domain.description: Domain to allow outbound requests to. Supports wildcards (e.g. '*.googleapis.com'). Use '*' to allow all domains.

transform.description: Headers to inject on all outbound requests matching this domain. Each entry is a flat {header_name: header_value} object. The egress proxy injects these automatically.

items.description: A single header to inject, e.g. {'Authorization': 'Bearer token'} or {'x-goog-api-key': 'key'}.

ModelOption.title: Model
ModelOption.description: The model that will complete your prompt.\n\nSee [models](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models) for additional details.

anyOf.description: An agentic capability model designed for direct interface interaction, allowing Gemini to perceive and navigate digital environments.
Our first hybrid reasoning model which supports a 1M token context window and has thinking budgets.
Our native image generation model, optimized for speed, flexibility, and contextual understanding. Text input and output is priced the same as 2.5 Flash.
Our smallest and most cost effective model, built for at scale usage.
The latest model based on Gemini 2.5 Flash lite optimized for cost-efficiency, high throughput and high quality.
Our native audio models optimized for higher quality audio outputs with better pacing, voice naturalness, verbosity, and mood.
The latest model based on the 2.5 Flash model. 2.5 Flash Preview is best for large scale processing, low-latency, high volume tasks that require thinking, and agentic use cases.
Our 2.5 Flash text-to-speech model optimized for powerful, low-latency controllable speech generation.
Our state-of-the-art multipurpose model, which excels at coding and complex reasoning tasks.
Our 2.5 Pro text-to-speech audio model optimized for powerful, low-latency speech generation for more natural outputs and easier to steer prompts.
Our most intelligent model built for speed, combining frontier intelligence with superior search and grounding.
State-of-the-art image generation and editing model.
Our most intelligent model with SOTA reasoning and multimodal understanding, and powerful agentic and vibe coding capabilities.
Our latest SOTA reasoning model with unprecedented depth and nuance, and powerful multimodal understanding and coding capabilities.
Pro-level visual intelligence with Flash-speed efficiency and reality-grounded generation capabilities.
Our most cost-efficient model, optimized for high-volume agentic tasks, translation, and simple data processing.
Our most cost-efficient model, optimized for high-volume agentic tasks, translation, and simple data processing.
Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: Powerful, low-latency speech generation. Enjoy natural outputs, steerable prompts, and new expressive audio tags for precise narration control.
Our low-latency, music generation model optimized for high-fidelity audio clips and precise rhythmic control.
Our advanced, full-song generative model with deep compositional understanding, optimized for precise structural control and complex transitions across diverse musical styles.
Our most intelligent model for sustained frontier performance in agentic and coding tasks.
Gemini Deep Research Agent
Gemini Deep Research Agent
Gemini Deep Research Max Agent
